claim: An independently implemented Cypher dialect with a tested openCypher-compatible subset and documented extensions; not a complete openCypher or Neo4j implementation.

status_definitions.covered: Implemented behavior is locked by KGLite-authored contract tests.
status_definitions.partial: Useful behavior exists, but a documented semantic or syntactic gap remains.
status_definitions.extension: KGLite behavior outside the claimed openCypher-compatible subset.
status_definitions.intentional_divergence: Supported behavior deliberately differs for KGLite's data model or runtime.

features.note: Core patterns are supported; relationship uniqueness for repeated and variable-length patterns is being aligned.
Null-extending optional pattern matches.
Predicates are broad; the remaining expression-level three-valued cases are tracked explicitly.
Projection, aliases, DISTINCT, and aggregation.
Projection, aggregation, wildcard preservation, and strict post-projection scope validation.
Stable multi-key ordering and pagination.
List expansion including null and empty-list behavior.
Duplicate-removing and duplicate-preserving set composition.
Node and relationship creation with inline properties.
Property, label, map merge, and mutable-property map replacement forms.
Entity deletion, detach deletion, and property/label removal.
Pattern merge and ON CREATE/ON MATCH reject null property keys before mutation.
Updating bodies and nested FOREACH are supported.
Read subqueries support correlated importing WITH; unit, write, UNION-body, and IN TRANSACTIONS forms are not supported.
Use the Python, Rust, or blueprint ingestion APIs.
Expression and predicate positions preserve three-valued results with standard precedence.
Membership preserves unknown for null operands and null-containing no-match lists.
List composition supports list/list and element/list forms; indexes and slice bounds require integers.
Quantifiers preserve unknown unless a decisive true/false result exists.
Simple and generic CASE forms.
Path values exist; exact relationship identity for parallel and incoming edges is tracked.
Returns KGLite logical node identity or stable relationship identity.
KGLite stores one immutable primary type plus additive secondary labels; labels(n) returns primary type first.
Embedded snapshots and optimistic commit checking replace a remote database transaction service.

extensions.note: KGLite functions and procedures accept canonical namespaced spellings; historical flat spellings remain equivalent.
Graph algorithms, rules, code analysis, and statistics procedures.
Pragmatic geometry and spatial helpers beyond the compatible subset.
Text normalization, matching, token, and distance helpers.
Post-aggregation filter accepted as a KGLite extension.
Window functions are a KGLite/GQL-style extension.
Additional set operators outside the claimed openCypher-compatible subset.
KGLite plan and execution diagnostics; output shape is KGLite-specific.
